query: WWDC 2024 2025 keynote Apple mentions ChatGPT OpenAI Google on stage history
answer:
Now I have comprehensive data from multiple sources. Let me compile the final research report.

---

## WWDC Keynote History: Apple Mentions of ChatGPT, OpenAI, and Google on Stage

### WWDC 2024 (June 10, 2024) — "Action Packed"

**The watershed moment for AI partnerships on Apple's stage.** Apple unveiled **Apple Intelligence**, its generative AI platform built into iOS 18, iPadOS 18, and macOS Sequoia. This keynote marked the **first time Apple explicitly named OpenAI and ChatGPT on the WWDC keynote stage**.

Key details:
- **Craig Federighi** (SVP Software Engineering) presented the Apple Intelligence suite and the ChatGPT integration.
- Apple announced a **system-wide integration with ChatGPT** (powered by **GPT-4o**), allowing Siri to hand off complex queries to ChatGPT with user permission.
- **Sam Altman** (OpenAI CEO) was present **in the audience** at Apple Park but did **not** present on stage.
- Apple emphasized privacy: ChatGPT requests are opt-in, IP addresses are obscured, no account is required, and OpenAI does not store requests.
- Apple noted it planned to support **other AI models** (such as Google Gemini) in the future, but did not feature Google as a partner at this event.
- The keynote architecture was deliberate: Apple's **on-device models** and **Private Cloud Compute** were presented first; ChatGPT was positioned as an optional external supplement — not the core of Apple Intelligence.

**Google at WWDC 2024:** Not a featured partner. Apple mentioned future plans to potentially integrate Google's Gemini, but Google was not highlighted on stage the way OpenAI was.

---

### WWDC 2025 (June 9, 2025) — "Sleek Peek"

Apple unveiled **iOS 26**, **macOS 26 (Tahoe)**, and a major version-numbering unification across all platforms. AI was again a major theme.

Key details:
- **ChatGPT integration was expanded**, not replaced:
  - **Visual Intelligence** now supports screenshot-based queries sent to ChatGPT
  - **AI Actions in Shortcuts** lets users choose an "engine": On-Device, Cloud (Private Cloud Compute), or **ChatGPT**
  - **Image Playground** integrates ChatGPT image generation models
  - **Enhanced Genmoji** combines existing emojis with ChatGPT integration
  - Writing Tools added ChatGPT "Compose" and "Describe your change" features
- Apple introduced the **Foundation Models Framework** for developers — enabling third-party Swift apps to integrate Apple Intelligence with as few as three lines of code.
- The three-tier AI model persisted: **On-Device → Private Cloud Compute → ChatGPT** as the selectable engines.
- **Google/Gemini was NOT announced as a partner at WWDC 2025.** ChatGPT/OpenAI remained the primary third-party AI integration.
- Craig Federighi again presented the macOS Tahoe announcement.

---

### Post-WWDC Developments (Late 2025 – Early 2026)

- **January 2026:** Apple and Google announced a **multi-year partnership** to incorporate **Google's Gemini models and cloud infrastructure** into Apple's next-generation foundation models.
- Apple reported on an earnings call that it was integrating an **on-device Gemini model into Siri**, as development of Apple's own model faced setbacks.
- The Gemini model Apple adopted has **1.2 trillion parameters** — significantly larger than Apple's internally developed models.
- **Early 2025:** Apple partnered with **Alibaba Group** to integrate Alibaba's **Qwen** large language models into Apple Intelligence for devices sold in **mainland China**.

---

### Historical Context: Google Mentions at WWDC Keynotes

| Year | Context | Nature |
|------|---------|--------|
| **WWDC 2007** | iPhone launch featured Google Maps & YouTube as built-in apps; Eric Schmidt appeared on stage with Steve Jobs | Positive partnership |
| **WWDC 2012** | Apple announced **Apple Maps** replacing Google Maps in iOS 6 | Competitive — moving away from Google |
| **Various years** | Safari benchmarked against Chrome (e.g., "faster than Chrome") | Indirect competitive reference |
| **WWDC 2024** | Future Gemini integration mentioned; Google not a featured partner | Brief mention |
| **WWDC 2025** | No notable Google mention; ChatGPT remained the AI partner | Absent |

### Historical Context: OpenAI/ChatGPT at WWDC

| Year | Mention |
|------|---------|
| **Pre-2024** | **Never mentioned** at any WWDC keynote |
| **WWDC 2024** | **First-ever mention** — featured partnership announcement with OpenAI for ChatGPT integration into Siri and Apple Intelligence |
| **WWDC 2025** | **Expanded** — deeper ChatGPT integrations across Visual Intelligence, Shortcuts, Image Playground, Genmoji |

---

### Key Observations

1. **WWDC 2024 was unprecedented**: Apple naming a specific AI company (OpenAI) and product (ChatGPT) on the keynote stage was a major departure from Apple's tradition of avoiding competitor/partner names.
2. **ChatGPT's rise forced Apple's hand**: Wikipedia notes that ChatGPT's late-2022 release "reportedly blindsided Apple executives" and forced a strategic refocus on AI.
3. **Three-tier AI architecture**: Apple established a consistent model — on-device processing first, Private Cloud Compute second, ChatGPT third — across both WWDC 2024 and 2025.
4. **Google Gemini came later**: The formal Apple-Google Gemini partnership was announced in **January 2026**, well after both WWDC events. It was not featured at either WWDC keynote.
5. **Apple's keynote culture**: Apple historically avoids naming competitors. The 2024 OpenAI partnership and the older 2007 Google partnership (Eric Schmidt on stage) are notable exceptions.

key_facts: WWDC 2024 (June 10, 2024) was the first time Apple explicitly named OpenAI and ChatGPT on the WWDC keynote stage, announcing a system-wide ChatGPT integration powered by GPT-4o as part of Apple Intelligence.
Craig Federighi presented the Apple Intelligence and ChatGPT integration at WWDC 2024; Sam Altman (OpenAI CEO) was in the audience but did not present on stage.

ChatGPT integration at WWDC 2024 was positioned as opt-in, privacy-preserving (IP obscured, no account required, OpenAI does not store requests), and supplementary to Apple's on-device and Private Cloud Compute models.

At WWDC 2025 (June 9, 2025, tagline 'Sleek Peek'), Apple expanded ChatGPT integrations into Visual Intelligence, Shortcuts (as a selectable AI engine), Image Playground, and Genmoji — OpenAI/ChatGPT remained the primary third-party AI partner.
Google/Gemini was NOT announced as a partner at either WWDC 2024 or WWDC 2025 keynotes. The Apple-Google Gemini multi-year partnership was announced in January 2026.
Apple's three-tier AI architecture persisted across both WWDCs: On-Device models → Private Cloud Compute → ChatGPT as the third-party external option.
WWDC 2025 introduced the Foundation Models Framework, allowing third-party developers to integrate Apple Intelligence with as few as three lines of Swift code.
ChatGPT's late-2022 release reportedly 'blindsided' Apple executives and forced a strategic refocus on AI, per Wikipedia's Apple Intelligence article.
At WWDC 2012, Apple announced Apple Maps replacing Google Maps in iOS 6 — one of the few times Google was prominently mentioned at a WWDC keynote.
In 2007, Google CEO Eric Schmidt appeared on stage at the iPhone launch event alongside Steve Jobs, representing one of the most prominent Google appearances at an Apple event.
In early 2025, Apple partnered with Alibaba Group to integrate Qwen LLMs into Apple Intelligence for mainland China devices.
The January 2026 Apple-Google deal involves Google's Gemini model with 1.2 trillion parameters being integrated into Siri, after Apple's own model development faced setbacks.
OpenAI and ChatGPT were never mentioned at any WWDC keynote prior to WWDC 2024.
